steps.action: Sign in as singleOwner and open the dashboard shell.
steps.expected: A single-team user should see only the current workspace indicator with no alternate switch target.
steps.observed: The session landed on http://dashboard.faworra.localhost:1355/dashboard and rendered the header label 'tenancy-ux-fedb5c00 Single Workspace' plus body text 'Workspace: tenancy-ux-fedb5c00 Single Workspace' with no dropdown menu or alternate workspace target exposed.

steps.action: Sign in as switcher and open the workspace dropdown.
steps.expected: A multi-team user should see the current workspace distinctly marked and at least one actionable alternate target.
steps.observed: The session landed on http://dashboard.faworra.localhost:1355/dashboard with trigger text 'tenancy-ux-fedb5c00 Switcher Primary Workspace'. Opening the dropdown showed a checked/current menu item for 'tenancy-ux-fedb5c00 Switcher Primary Workspace' and an actionable alternate item 'tenancy-ux-fedb5c00 Switcher Secondary Workspace'.

evidence.network: Both login sessions observed GET http://api.faworra.localhost:1355/api/auth/get-session -> 200, GET http://api.faworra.localhost:1355/trpc/viewer,privateData?batch=1&input=%7B%7D -> 200, and GET http://api.faworra.localhost:1355/trpc/team.list?batch=1&input=%7B%7D -> 200.

assertions.title: Team switching updates the active workspace and persists

steps.action: Sign in as switcher and capture the starting dashboard state.
steps.expected: The shell and protected data should both reflect the primary workspace before switching.
steps.observed: Before switching, the header trigger and dashboard body both showed 'tenancy-ux-fedb5c00 Switcher Primary Workspace'.

steps.action: Choose 'tenancy-ux-fedb5c00 Switcher Secondary Workspace' from the dropdown.
steps.expected: The active workspace should update immediately in both the shell and protected data after the switch mutation succeeds.
steps.observed: The switch mutation POST http://api.faworra.localhost:1355/trpc/user.switchTeam?batch=1 returned 200 and the header trigger changed to 'tenancy-ux-fedb5c00 Switcher Secondary Workspace', but the immediate post-switch dashboard body still rendered 'Workspace: tenancy-ux-fedb5c00 Switcher Primary Workspace'.

steps.action: Wait for the UI to settle, then refresh and revisit a protected dashboard URL.
steps.expected: The newly selected workspace should persist across reload and protected navigation without re-login.
steps.observed: After ~2 seconds the body updated to the secondary workspace. A reload on /dashboard and a revisit to http://dashboard.faworra.localhost:1355/dashboard?tab=tenancy-check both continued to render 'tenancy-ux-fedb5c00 Switcher Secondary Workspace'.

evidence.network: POST http://api.faworra.localhost:1355/trpc/user.switchTeam?batch=1 -> 200, followed by GET http://api.faworra.localhost:1355/trpc/viewer,team.list,privateData?batch=1&input=%7B%7D -> 200. Reload and protected revisit kept the session on /dashboard and /dashboard?tab=tenancy-check with secondary-workspace data.

assertions.issues: Persistence worked, but the switch did not update shell and protected data immediately: right after the successful user.switchTeam mutation, the shell showed the secondary workspace while the dashboard body still showed the primary workspace.

assertions.title: Team switch state stays consistent across shell and protected queries

steps.action: Switch the authenticated switcher session from the primary workspace to the secondary workspace.
steps.expected: After the switch, the protected shell and protected data should agree on the same newly selected workspace.
steps.observed: Immediately after POST /trpc/user.switchTeam?batch=1 -> 200, the header trigger showed 'tenancy-ux-fedb5c00 Switcher Secondary Workspace' while the body still showed 'Workspace: tenancy-ux-fedb5c00 Switcher Primary Workspace'.

steps.action: Refresh the page and revisit a protected dashboard URL.
steps.expected: Refresh and subsequent protected navigation should continue to agree on the selected workspace.
steps.observed: After the delayed re-render, a reload and revisit to http://dashboard.faworra.localhost:1355/dashboard?tab=tenancy-check both rendered the secondary workspace consistently in the shell/body.

evidence.network: POST http://api.faworra.localhost:1355/trpc/user.switchTeam?batch=1 -> 200, then GET http://api.faworra.localhost:1355/trpc/viewer,team.list,privateData?batch=1&input=%7B%7D -> 200. The later reload/revisit requests continued to return secondary-workspace content.

assertions.issues: The post-switch shell and protected data were briefly inconsistent: the header updated to the secondary workspace before the dashboard body/current-team content caught up.

assertions.title: Teamless authenticated sessions are sent to the correct recovery surface

steps.expected: An authenticated user with no accepted workspace should be sent to the recovery surface instead of remaining on /login or entering a broken dashboard shell.
steps.observed: After sign-in, the session settled on http://dashboard.faworra.localhost:1355/teams and rendered the invite-recovery surface with the pending invite card for 'tenancy-ux-fedb5c00 Single Workspace'.

steps.action: While still authenticated and teamless, open /dashboard directly.
steps.expected: Protected entry should route back to the same recovery surface.
steps.observed: Opening http://dashboard.faworra.localhost:1355/dashboard returned to http://dashboard.faworra.localhost:1355/teams rather than rendering a broken protected shell.

evidence.consoleErrors: none before invite actions were triggered
evidence.network: GET http://api.faworra.localhost:1355/api/auth/get-session -> 200, GET http://api.faworra.localhost:1355/trpc/viewer,teamInvites.invitesByEmail?batch=1&input=%7B%7D -> 200, and GET http://api.faworra.localhost:1355/trpc/team.list?batch=1&input=%7B%7D -> 200 while the authenticated teamless session resolved to /teams.

assertions.title: Teamless users with pending invites can recover through the invite surface

steps.action: Sign in as browserRecipient and inspect /teams.
steps.expected: The teamless recovery surface should show the pending invite and expose actionable Accept / Decline controls.
steps.observed: http://dashboard.faworra.localhost:1355/teams rendered the invite card for 'tenancy-ux-fedb5c00 Single Workspace' with visible Accept and Decline buttons.

steps.action: Accept the pending invite as browserRecipient.
steps.expected: The invite should be actionable from the recovery surface and create a usable workspace session.
steps.observed: POST http://api.faworra.localhost:1355/trpc/teamInvites.accept?batch=1 returned 200, GET http://api.faworra.localhost:1355/trpc/privateData?batch=1&input=%7B%7D returned 200, and the session settled on http://dashboard.faworra.localhost:1355/dashboard with body text 'Workspace: tenancy-ux-fedb5c00 Single Workspace' and 'API: This is private'.

steps.action: Sign in as browserDeclineRecipient and inspect /teams.
steps.expected: The decline-path recipient should also see the pending invite on the same recovery surface.
steps.observed: http://dashboard.faworra.localhost:1355/teams rendered the same single-workspace invite with Accept and Decline controls for the decline recipient.

steps.action: Decline the pending invite as browserDeclineRecipient.
steps.expected: Once the invite list is exhausted, the teamless recovery flow should route to onboarding.
steps.observed: POST http://api.faworra.localhost:1355/trpc/teamInvites.decline?batch=1 returned 200, GET http://api.faworra.localhost:1355/trpc/teamInvites.invitesByEmail?batch=1&input=%7B%7D returned 200, and the session settled on http://dashboard.faworra.localhost:1355/onboarding once the invite list was empty.

evidence.consoleErrors: After the accept and decline transitions, agent-browser surfaced 'Switched to client rendering because the server rendering errored: Authentication required'. The accept and decline actions still completed.
evidence.network: GET http://api.faworra.localhost:1355/trpc/viewer,teamInvites.invitesByEmail?batch=1&input=%7B%7D -> 200, POST http://api.faworra.localhost:1355/trpc/teamInvites.accept?batch=1 -> 200, GET http://api.faworra.localhost:1355/trpc/privateData?batch=1&input=%7B%7D -> 200, POST http://api.faworra.localhost:1355/trpc/teamInvites.decline?batch=1 -> 200, and GET http://api.faworra.localhost:1355/trpc/teamInvites.invitesByEmail?batch=1&input=%7B%7D -> 200.

assertions.title: Authenticated teamless protected entry routes into the correct recovery surface

steps.action: Use the authenticated teamless browserRecipient session to open /dashboard.
steps.expected: The protected route should send the user into the invite/team recovery surface instead of a broken dashboard shell.
steps.observed: The browser opened http://dashboard.faworra.localhost:1355/dashboard and settled on http://dashboard.faworra.localhost:1355/teams with the readable invite-recovery UI still present.

evidence.consoleErrors: none before invite actions were triggered
evidence.network: The authenticated teamless session continued to use GET http://api.faworra.localhost:1355/api/auth/get-session -> 200 plus GET http://api.faworra.localhost:1355/trpc/viewer,teamInvites.invitesByEmail?batch=1&input=%7B%7D -> 200 while the protected dashboard entry resolved back to /teams.

assertions.title: Invite-driven browser recovery reaches a usable dashboard session

steps.action: Sign in as browserRecipient and recover through /teams.
steps.expected: The recipient should be able to accept the pending invite and land in a usable dashboard session where the shell and current-team data agree.
steps.observed: The recipient reached http://dashboard.faworra.localhost:1355/teams with the pending invite for 'tenancy-ux-fedb5c00 Single Workspace'.

steps.action: Accept the invite and inspect the resulting dashboard shell.
steps.expected: The final dashboard should show the invited workspace consistently in the protected shell and the protected data.
steps.observed: The session eventually settled on http://dashboard.faworra.localhost:1355/dashboard and the dashboard body showed 'Workspace: tenancy-ux-fedb5c00 Single Workspace' with private data, but the top shell no longer displayed any current-workspace indicator at all. The interactive snapshot after accept contained only Home, Dashboard, Toggle theme, and the user menu.

steps.action: Check browser errors on the final accepted dashboard state.
steps.expected: The invite-driven transition should reach the usable dashboard without server-render/auth errors.
steps.observed: agent-browser errors reported 'Switched to client rendering because the server rendering errored: Authentication required' twice on the final accepted-dashboard transition.

evidence.consoleErrors: Switched to client rendering because the server rendering errored: Authentication required (reported twice after invite acceptance).
evidence.network: POST http://api.faworra.localhost:1355/trpc/teamInvites.accept?batch=1 -> 200 and GET http://api.faworra.localhost:1355/trpc/privateData?batch=1&input=%7B%7D -> 200. The final dashboard body rendered the invited workspace, but the protected shell did not show the same current-workspace indicator.

assertions.issues: Invite acceptance reached a dashboard body with the invited workspace, but the protected shell failed to render the current-workspace indicator and the transition emitted Authentication required server-render errors.

frictions.description: agent-browser network requests returned 'No requests captured' for these localhost flows even though the app was making API calls.
frictions.resolution: Used performance.getEntriesByType('resource') inside each browser session to record the relevant auth, team-list, switch, invite, and protected-data request URLs and status codes.

frictions.description: Several router transitions lagged behind API completion: immediate post-login URL reads still showed /login, the switcher body lagged the shell, and invite accept/decline needed extra settle time before the final route appeared.
frictions.resolution: Added explicit extra waits and captured both immediate and settled screenshots/observations so the report could distinguish transient mismatches from the final settled state.

summary: Tested 7 browser tenancy assertions for tenancy-ux-parity: 4 passed and 3 failed. Single-team and multi-team switcher affordances behaved as expected, teamless recovery routed correctly to /teams, and invite accept/decline actions worked; however the switch flow briefly left shell/private data out of sync (failing VAL-TENANCY-004 and VAL-CROSS-004), and the accepted-invite dashboard ended with shell/current-workspace mismatch plus Authentication required render errors (failing VAL-CROSS-007).
